outbound_official.note: No official Guinea government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data. Checked mae.gov.gn (Ministry of Foreign Affairs), paf.gov.gn (Police aux Frontières / DCPAF visa portal), gouvernement.gov.gn, service-public.gov.gn — all focus on inbound visa requirements for foreign nationals entering Guinea, not on where Guinean ordinary-passport holders can travel without a prior consular visa.

visa_free.notes: Visa exempt per PAF e-visa portal country selector (code 2 = no visa required). Max stay duration not stated on official page.

Visa-free, 30-day maximum stay. Confirmed by Singapore Ministry of Foreign Affairs official travel advisory page (mfa.gov.sg): 'Singaporeans may stay up to 30 days in Guinea without a visa.' Prior record had max_stay_days as null; corrected to 30.

visa_on_arrival.notes: UAE nationals may obtain a visa on arrival in Guinea for a maximum stay of 90 days. Confirmed by multiple secondary sources (VisaHQ, Wikipedia Visa policy of Guinea) citing the PAF portal category assignment. No dedicated Guinea official page could be directly fetched to show the exact portal code, but PAF portal assigns a special code (code 1, as previously noted for USA and Canada) to UAE as well. UAE was not in the prior record; added based on corroborating secondary sources consistent with PAF system.

PAF e-visa portal assigns code 1 (distinct from standard e-visa code 0 and visa-free code 2). Multiple secondary sources report that Canadian citizens receive an e-visa with exceptionally long validity (up to 5 years) rather than a standard 90-day e-visa. Exact entitlement (direct visa on arrival vs. extended e-visa tier) remains ambiguous; recorded under visa_on_arrival with caveat pending official confirmation of the 5-year extended validity claim.

PAF e-visa portal assigns code 1 (distinct from standard e-visa code 0 and visa-free code 2). Multiple secondary sources report that US citizens receive an e-visa with exceptionally long validity (up to 5 years) rather than a standard 90-day e-visa. US State Department advisory says 'a visa is required' without specifying visa on arrival. Exact entitlement remains ambiguous; recorded under visa_on_arrival with caveat pending official confirmation.

e_visa.notes: The PAF portal designates all countries not in the visa-free (code 2) or special (code 1) category as requiring an e-visa. Visa types: Tourist Visa (USD 80, max 90 days), Transborderment Visa (USD 50, max 3 days), Transit Visa (USD 50, max 3 days), Long Term Visa/VLS (USD 150, 1 year), Diplomatic/Service/Courtesy (VDSC, fee per approval letter). Process: apply online at paf.gov.gn/visa, receive Entry Visa Letter, collect visa at border arrival counter. Processing time: 72 hours.

visa_policy.notes: Guinea operates an e-visa system branded as 'ETA Electronic Travel Authorisation of GUINEA' via paf.gov.gn/visa (Police aux Frontières / DCPAF portal). All 15 ECOWAS member states are visa-free (90-day max stay per APIP). Non-ECOWAS visa-free countries (Algeria, Egypt, Morocco, Namibia, Rwanda, Tunisia) confirmed via PAF portal code 2; max stay not stated. Singapore confirmed visa-free 30 days per Singapore MFA official advisory. UAE nationals confirmed for visa on arrival (90 days) per multiple consistent secondary sources citing PAF portal. USA and Canada are assigned a special portal code (code 1) distinct from the standard e-visa — secondary sources describe this as an extended-validity e-visa (up to 5 years). The portal is maintained by the Ministry of Security and Civil Protection, Government of Guinea.

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of Chinese diplomatic passports, service passports, and passports for public affairs (official) do not require a visa for Guinea for a maximum stay of 30 days. Agreement on mutual visa exemption effective 16 September 2017. Nationals holding ordinary Chinese passports endorsed 'for public affairs' are also covered for 30-day visa-free stay.

Holders of South African diplomatic, official, and service passports do not require a visa for Guinea for a maximum stay of 90 days. Based on bilateral visa waiver agreement signed in Pretoria on 24 November 2006, entered into force 23 January 2007 (UN Treaty No. 45066).

cbi.notes: No citizenship-by-investment program found on any official Guinea government source. Searches of paf.gov.gn, mae.gov.gn, and related government domains returned no such program.

validation_notes: CORRECTION: Singapore max_stay_days updated from null to 30, confirmed by Singapore MFA official advisory (mfa.gov.sg): 'Singaporeans may stay up to 30 days in Guinea without a visa.'

ADDITION: UAE (ARE) added to visa_on_arrival with 90-day max stay, confirmed by multiple corroborating secondary sources (Wikipedia 'Visa policy of Guinea', VisaHQ) citing PAF portal category. UAE was entirely absent from prior record.

RETENTION: USA and Canada remain in visa_on_arrival (PAF portal code 1) with null max_stay_days and caveat; secondary sources describe an extended e-visa (up to 5 years validity) rather than a traditional VOA, but the PAF code 1 classification is not standard e-visa code 0, so classification under visa_on_arrival is retained pending clearer official documentation.

RETENTION: Diplomatic/service passport exemptions for Romania, Russia, Zimbabwe, and Turkey remain unrecorded in conditional_access as no official Guinea, Romanian, Russian, Zimbabwean, or Turkish government source confirmed these specific bilateral arrangements. Multiple non-official aggregators report them (90 days for Romania/Russia/Zimbabwe diplomatic+service, 90 days for Turkey diplomatic only).

RETENTION: China (30 days, diplomatic/service/official, per Chinese NIA en.nia.gov.cn) and South Africa (90 days, diplomatic/service/official, per DIRCO dirco.gov.za) remain in conditional_access, unchanged.
NO CHANGE: CBI, RBI, and fast_track remain empty — no official programs found.
SOURCE UPDATE: Singapore MFA page added as official source for Singapore visa-free confirmation.
gaps: Max stay days for non-ECOWAS visa-free nationals (Algeria, Egypt, Morocco, Namibia, Rwanda, Tunisia) not stated on any official Guinea page.

The meaning of PAF portal code 1 (assigned to USA, Canada, and possibly UAE) is not explicitly documented on any accessible official Guinea page — may indicate visa on arrival, extended-validity e-visa, or a legacy category. US State Department page states 'a visa is required' without specifying VOA. Secondary sources claim 5-year validity for US/Canada e-visas, unconfirmed from official Guinea sources.

Diplomatic/service passport visa exemptions reportedly exist for Romania, Russia, Zimbabwe (90 days), and Turkey (90 days diplomatic only) per Wikipedia and travel aggregators, but no official Guinea, Romanian, Russian, Zimbabwean, or Turkish government source could be confirmed. NOT recorded per official-source-first rule.
No RBI / golden visa / investor-residence program found on official sources.
No fast-track / talent / digital-nomad / startup visa program found on official sources.
No CBI program found on official sources.

No credential-based (Type A) conditional entry rules found on any official Guinea government source — no evidence that holders of US visas, Schengen visas, UK visas, GCC visas, or other foreign residence permits receive any preferential entry to Guinea beyond their ordinary nationality-based entitlement.
No OCI or other special diaspora status conditional entry rules found on any official Guinea source.
The paf.gov.gn WordPress main site returns 500 errors on some subpages, limiting direct access to the full country-code listing from the visa application portal.
